summary: Partially decompiled and matched the library unit `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c`, adding 17 fully matched functions and one partial improvement according to the decomp.dev bot. The PR also moved and refined color-overlay-related struct definitions into `lb/types.h`, replaced fighter-specific placeholder `ftDeviceUnk2` usage with `ColorOverlay`, introduced typed static globals/locals for `lb_00F9`, and corrected symbol scopes/names for newly localized constants and `cur_data`.
what_changed: The bot report recorded 17 new full matches in `main/melee/lb/lb_00F9` totaling +1076 matched bytes and a global matched-code increase of +0.03%, plus `lb_80013F78` improved to 73.33%.

Moved `ColorOverlay` out of `src/common_structs.h` into `src/melee/lb/types.h`, where it is used with more precise fields such as `x28_colanim` as a union of integer id and pointer, `x74_light_rot_x`, and `x78_light_rot_yz`.
Removed placeholder fighter-local structs `ftDeviceUnk1` and `ftDeviceUnk2` from `ft/types.h` and changed Fighter fields `x408`, `x488`, and `x508` to `ColorOverlay`.

Updated `ftCo_800C0658` and `ftCo_800C0674` to return `ColorOverlay*` rather than `ftDeviceUnk2*`, and updated users to access `x28_colanim.i` or `.ptr` depending on whether the field is used as an id or pointer.
Adjusted item code to use `struct Fighter_804D653C_t*` for `it_804D6D04` and to index color-animation data through `item_data->x548_colorOverlay.x28_colanim.i`.

Added local/static declarations in `lb_00F9.static.h`, including typed buffers `lb_804D63A0`, `lb_804D63A8`, free-list global `cur_data`, rumble data pointer `lb_804D63C0`, helper structs `lb_80013FF0_t` and `lb_804D63A0_t`, and static prototypes for local helpers.
Updated `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to mark several strings/floats/SJIS constants as local `@NNN` labels and renamed sbss `lb_804D63A4` to `cur_data`.
Added specific includes needed by the new implementations, including `baselib/rumble.h`, `baselib/tobj.h`, `dolphin/pad.h`, `lb/lbarchive.h`, and `dolphin/gx.h`.

Updated declarations and documentation in `lb_00F9.h` and `lb_00F9.dox`, including typed signatures for `lb_8000FD18`, `lb_800100B0`, `lb_800122C8`, `fn_800138AC`, `lb_80013F78`, `lb_80014498`, and `lb_800144C8`.
smart_moves: Centralized the real `ColorOverlay` layout in `lb/types.h` instead of leaving duplicate or misleading definitions in generic `common_structs.h` and fighter-specific placeholder types.
Used a union for `ColorOverlay.x28_colanim` because the same storage is observed as both a color-animation id and a pointer, letting callers express intent without changing the offset.
Kept helper-only functions and globals in `lb_00F9.static.h`, preserving public header cleanliness while still giving the C file enough type information to match.
Renamed only the sbss symbol with meaningful evidence (`cur_data`) while converting compiler-local constants to `@NNN` local symbols rather than over-naming anonymous data.

Matched small wrapper functions directly against known library calls, e.g. `lb_800122C8` wrapping `HSD_ImageDescCopyFromEFB`, `fn_800138AC` wrapping `HSD_Free`, and rumble helpers wrapping `HSD_PadRumble*`.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` and explicit stack helper structs for `lb_800119DC` and `lb_80011A50`, showing a matching tactic for functions whose stack layout matters.
Preserved comments where uncertainty remains, such as the `ftCo_800C0694` note about a wrong return type/union, instead of pretending the type question is fully resolved.
decomp_lessons: When a struct is shared across fighter, item, and library code, place it in the subsystem header that owns the concept (`lb/types.h` here) and update all cross-system users to the same type.
If one field is used both as an integer index and as a pointer in different contexts, a named union can improve readability and matching without disturbing the binary layout.
Moving anonymous literals in `symbols.txt` from global `lbl_`/`lb_` names to local `@NNN` labels can be part of a correct match when the compiler emitted local constants.
Small exact-match wrappers are valuable: even one-line wrappers to HSD routines or rumble APIs contributed to the decomp.dev match count.
For partially matched files, it is useful to type static globals and private helper structs in a `.static.h` rather than polluting exported headers.
Replacing placeholder types like `ftDeviceUnk2` with a more semantically accurate existing struct can unlock matches in unrelated files that access the same offsets.

Bitfield names should be refined from generic `x7C_bN` only where usage supports semantics; this PR kept `x7C_flag2` for uncertain behavior but used `x7C_light_enable` where lighting behavior was evidenced.
naming_conventions: Use `ColorOverlay` for the shared color animation/light overlay struct instead of `ftDeviceUnk2`.
Use `ColorOverlay_UnkInner` for the pointed-to inner object behind `ColorOverlay.x28_colanim.ptr` when only sparse fields such as `x7B` are known.
Use a union member pair `x28_colanim.i` and `x28_colanim.ptr` to distinguish integer id/index access from pointer access at offset 0x28.
Use semantic light-field names `x50_light_color`, `x74_light_rot_x`, and `x78_light_rot_yz` where callsites manipulate `HSD_LObj` color and rotation-derived position.
Use `Fighter_804D653C_t` for the global table type used by color-animation/rumble-related code, with fields `unk`, `unk4`, and `unk5` pending stronger names.
Compiler-local constants in the symbol map may be named with local labels such as `@17`, `@174`, `@206` and given `scope:local` instead of artificial global `lbl_`/`lb_` names.
The free-list global at `.sbss:0x804D63A4` was named `cur_data` based on its use in `lb_8000FD18`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` around stack-local struct initialization to reproduce stack layout in `lb_800119DC` and `lb_80011A50`.

Introduced exact-size static buffer wrapper structs with `STATIC_ASSERT`, e.g. `lb_804D63A0_t` size `0xBE00` and `lb_804D63A8_t` size `0x1C0`, to type static allocation globals without changing layout.
Matched floating-point comparison behavior in `lb_800103D8` with explicit casts to `double` for comparisons against `0.0`, which can affect generated PowerPC code.
Used bit extraction from packed script data in `lb_80013F78` with shifts and masks to recover light rotation fields while preserving match-sensitive operations.
Converted static globals from generic `UNK_T` definitions in the C file to typed static declarations in `.static.h`, aligning symbol storage with the compiler's static/local expectations.
Corrected symbol scopes for constants to local `@NNN` entries in `symbols.txt`, a symbol-map tactic often needed once functions/data are matched closely enough.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported: matched code increased to 38.52% (+0.03%, +1076 bytes), with 17 new full matches in `main/melee/lb/lb_00F9` and one partial improvement (`lb_80013F78` to 73.33%).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching `lb_80013F78` or remaining `lb_00F9` placeholders.
Search for subsequent renames of `Fighter_804D653C_t`, `ColorOverlay_UnkInner`, or `cur_data` to see whether stronger semantics were established.
Look for PRs that further refine `ColorOverlay` bitfields, especially `x7C_flag2`, `x7C_color_enable`, and `x7C_light_enable`.
Search for additional uses of `x28_colanim.i` versus `x28_colanim.ptr` to validate the union pattern across item/fighter/library code.
Check whether any later cleanup removes the stale `ftDeviceUnk2` forward typedef still added in `lb/forward.h` despite removing the struct from `ft/types.h`.
